title: Magnet Pole Finder — Fig. 4.17
subject: Science
textbook_reference: NCERT Curiosity — Grade 6 Science, Chapter 4, Fig. 4.17

diagram.description: Three bar magnets arranged in an L-shape. Magnet 1 and Magnet 2 are horizontal; Magnet 3 is vertical.

steps.ruleExplanation: Both ends of the same magnet always have OPPOSITE poles. One end is N, the other must be S.
steps.hint: Ends 5 & 6 are both ends of Magnet 3. End 5 = N, so End 6 must be the OPPOSITE!
steps.wrongAnswerHint: Remember: Same magnet = OPPOSITE poles! One end is N, the other must be S.

steps.ruleExplanation: When two magnet ends touch and ATTRACT each other, they must be UNLIKE poles (one N and one S).
steps.hint: Ends 4 & 5 are touching and ATTRACT each other. End 5 = N. Unlike poles attract — so End 4 is...?
steps.wrongAnswerHint: Remember: ATTRACT means UNLIKE poles → if one end is N, the other must be S!

steps.ruleExplanation: Both ends of the same magnet always have OPPOSITE poles.
steps.hint: Ends 3 & 4 are both ends of Magnet 2. We found End 4 = S, so End 3 must be the OPPOSITE!
steps.wrongAnswerHint: Remember: Same magnet = OPPOSITE poles! End 4 is S, so End 3 must be N.

steps.ruleExplanation: When two magnet ends touch and ATTRACT each other, they must be UNLIKE poles.
steps.hint: Ends 2 & 3 are touching and ATTRACT each other. End 3 = N. Unlike poles attract — so End 2 is...?
steps.wrongAnswerHint: Remember: ATTRACT means UNLIKE poles → if one end is N, the other must be S!

steps.ruleExplanation: Both ends of the same magnet always have OPPOSITE poles.
steps.hint: Ends 1 & 2 are both ends of Magnet 1. We found End 2 = S, so End 1 must be the OPPOSITE!
steps.wrongAnswerHint: Remember: Same magnet = OPPOSITE poles! End 2 is S, so End 1 must be N.

sameMagnetRule.name: Same Magnet Rule
sameMagnetRule.description: The two ends of the same bar magnet always have OPPOSITE poles. If one end is North (N), the other end must be South (S), and vice versa.
sameMagnetRule.studentFriendly: Same magnet → opposite poles! It's like a see-saw — one side up, other side down.

junctionAttractRule.name: Junction Attract Rule
junctionAttractRule.description: When two ends of different magnets are touching and attract each other, they must be UNLIKE poles (North attracts South, South attracts North).
junctionAttractRule.studentFriendly: Friends are different! N and S attract each other. Same poles push away.

learningObjectives: Understand that every magnet has two poles: North (N) and South (S)
Apply the 'Same Magnet Rule': ends of the same magnet always have opposite poles
Apply the 'Junction Attract Rule': magnets that attract must have unlike poles touching
Work systematically through a chain of magnets to determine all pole positions
instructions_for_student: Start from End 5 which is marked N (already given!). At each step, use the junction arrow or same-magnet rule to figure out what the nearby end must be. Select N or S — correct labels lock in and turn coloured. Complete all 5 blanks to finish!
